mode.title: Mode
mode.serverSummary: Local MCP / CLI server is available on port {port}.
mode.managedClientRunning: Managed client runtime is polling the remote backend.
mode.managedClientMcpWsRunning: Managed client runtime is connected to the remote desktop WebSocket endpoint.
mode.managedClientWaiting: Managed client runtime is enabled and waiting for setup to start.
mode.closeHint: Closing the window only hides it to the tray. Use tray Quit to fully stop the app.
mode.closeHintManaged: Closing the window does not stop managed client polling. Use tray Quit to fully stop the client.

dashboard.title: Dashboard
dashboard.pullInactiveHint: Available when running in managed client mode.
dashboard.pullWaitingMcpWs: Connected and waiting for tool calls
dashboard.connectionStateSigninHint: Sign in to establish the bridge
dashboard.connectionIdPending: Connection ID will appear after the bridge is established.
dashboard.noRunningSessions: No live local sessions right now.
dashboard.noSessions: No sessions yet. Use MCP tools or WebSocket to execute commands.
dashboard.clearHistoryRequiresSignin: Sign in first before clearing history in managed MCP WebSocket mode.

activities.title: Activities
activities.clearConfirm: This will permanently delete all activity records. Continue?

builtInTools.title: Built-in Tools
builtInTools.description: Manage local safety rules for the built-in shell and file read tools exposed by the desktop client.
builtInTools.currentPermissionProfileDescription: Permission profile selection is managed on the dedicated Permissions page. This page is now for advanced per-tool restrictions and overrides.
builtInTools.permissionProfileDescription: Choose the remote exposure model first. The profile sets which desktop tools are published upstream and how much successful tool output can leave the machine.
builtInTools.permissionProfileCommandOnlySummary: Remote side can trigger local commands, but successful tool calls only return success status. File reads, output reads, and all external MCP servers stay blocked.
builtInTools.permissionProfileInteractiveTrustedSumma: Remote side can use a small interactive handle surface such as session_create and session_wait, but successful results still avoid returning command output or file contents.
builtInTools.permissionProfileFullLocalAdminSummary: Full local tool surface, including unrestricted file reads and both HTTP and stdio external MCP servers.
builtInTools.permissionProfileDemoSummary: All security checks disabled. Full tool surface with no allowlists, no output redaction, and no size limits. For demos and local testing only — do not use in production.
builtInTools.permissionProfileOverridesActive: This profile currently has manual overrides below. Saving will persist only the differences from the selected profile.
builtInTools.permissionProfileNoOverrides: The current settings match the selected profile defaults.
builtInTools.loadFailed: Failed to load built-in tool configuration, showing defaults: {error}
builtInTools.saveApplied: Built-in tool rules saved and republished with {toolCount} tool(s).
builtInTools.savePendingPublishRuntimeInactive: Built-in tool rules saved, but managed MCP WebSocket mode is not currently running, so tools were not republished.
builtInTools.savePendingPublishBridgeNotReady: Built-in tool rules saved, but the managed MCP WebSocket bridge is not ready to republish tools yet. The latest rules will be used on the next successful connection.
builtInTools.savePendingPublishRepublishPending: Built-in tool rules were saved. Tool republish is still running in the background, so the UI returned immediately instead of waiting for slow external MCP servers.
builtInTools.saveFailed: Failed to save built-in tool rules: {error}
builtInTools.invalidNumericConfig: All numeric limits must be positive integers.
builtInTools.allowlistTooltip: Empty allowlist = not tightened. Non-empty allowlist = strict enforcement.
builtInTools.shellAllowlistHint: Use allowlists as the primary boundary. In managed-client-mcp-ws mode, shell_execute requires at least one allowed executable name AND at least one allowed working directory.
builtInTools.shellAllowedDirectories: Allowed working directories (one per line, required in managed-client-mcp-ws mode)
builtInTools.shellRecommendedTemplateHint: Recommended starter template: common project tools such as git, node, npm, npx, and python.
builtInTools.shellAllowlistRequiredManagedMcpWsTitle: Shell execution cannot be saved yet
builtInTools.shellAllowlistRequiredManagedMcpWs: In managed-client-mcp-ws mode, shell_execute requires at least one allowed executable name and at least one allowed working directory.
builtInTools.shellAllowlistRequiredManagedMcpWsBody: Shell execution is enabled, but the allowed executable list or working directory list is empty. Add at least one entry to each, or use the recommended template before saving.
builtInTools.shellChainingBlockedHint: Command chaining (;, &&, ||) is automatically blocked when pipes are disabled.
builtInTools.shellAllowPathsOutsideWorkspace: Allow paths outside workspace in command arguments
builtInTools.shellSandboxHint: Sandbox mode strips credential-like environment variables (tokens, keys, secrets) and redirects HOME/TEMP to the workspace directory, limiting data accessible to executed processes.
builtInTools.fileReadAllowlistHint: Use allowed read roots as the primary boundary. In full-local-admin mode, file_read requires at least one allowed read root.
builtInTools.fileReadRecommendedTemplateHint: Recommended starter template: the current managed workspace root and current working directory.
builtInTools.fileReadAllowlistRequiredFullLocalAdmin: In full-local-admin mode, file_read requires at least one allowed read root.
builtInTools.fileReadProfileRestricted: File read is reserved for full-local-admin. In command-only and interactive-trusted modes, successful tool calls must not return local file contents to the remote side.
builtInTools.managedMcpServerAdminDescription: Enable the built-in desktop tool that allows trusted remote instructions to create or update external MCP server configuration on this node. Servers created remotely default to 'experimental' trust level — you must manually promote the trust level in the External MCP Servers page before they can be actively published.
builtInTools.managedMcpServerAdminSandboxStdioHint: Sandbox mode strips credential-like environment variables from stdio MCP server processes and redirects HOME/TEMP into the workspace. Explicit env entries in the server config are preserved.
builtInTools.managedMcpServerAdminAllowedStdioServerC: Allowed stdio server commands (one per line)
When non-empty, only these executables may be used as stdio MCP server commands. Leave empty to allow any command.
builtInTools.managedMcpServerAdminProfileRestricted: Managed MCP server administration is reserved for full-local-admin because it expands the node's remote attack surface.
builtInTools.managedMcpServerAdminNote: This publishes the remote_configure_mcp_server tool upstream in managed MCP WebSocket mode. HTTP is lower risk than stdio because stdio can launch local processes.

permissions.title: Permissions
permissions.description: Choose the remote exposure model for this desktop node before tuning individual tool restrictions.
permissions.previewDescription: Selecting a permission profile updates the live preview below so you can see which desktop tools will be published upstream and which ones stay blocked.
permissions.emptyExternalMcpList: No matching MCP servers in the current saved configuration.
permissions.externalMcpBlockedTransport: Blocked by the selected profile transport policy.

transportDescription.http: Connects to an external MCP server over HTTP.
transportDescription.stdio: Launches or talks to an external MCP server through standard input/output.

toolResultModeDescription.status-only: Only status metadata is returned. Output content is not returned directly.
toolResultModeDescription.handle: Returns a handle or session reference. Use follow-up tools to read details.
toolResultModeDescription.full: Returns the full tool result, including the main output payload.

permissions.advancedDescription: Detailed shell, file read, and managed MCP server restrictions remain on the Built-in Tools page.
permissions.fullLocalAdminWarning: Warning: Full Local Admin grants unrestricted shell execution, file reads, and external MCP server access. All remote tool calls can read and modify any file on this machine without restriction. Use only if you fully trust the remote side.
permissions.demoWarning: ⚠️ DEMO MODE — All security checks are disabled. Every remote tool call has unrestricted access to this machine: shell execution without allowlists, any file read, no output redaction. Never use this profile in a production or shared environment.

settings.title: Settings
settings.mcpDescription: Connect MCP clients to this server with this URL.
settings.accountDescription: Manage the current managed session on this device.
settings.workspaceDescription: Default location for files created by local tools and local MCP servers. By default, it sits next to the app entry.
settings.signInConfigMissing: Managed MCP WebSocket base URL is not configured yet.
settings.externalMcpDescription: Configure additional MCP servers and publish their tools through the managed MCP WebSocket client.
settings.externalMcpPermissionsDescription: Permission profile selection is managed on the dedicated Permissions page.
settings.externalMcpAllowedTransportsCommandOnly: External MCP publication is disabled in command-only mode.
settings.externalMcpAllowedTransportsInteractiveT: External MCP publication stays disabled in interactive-trusted mode. Only the small built-in interactive handle surface is allowed.
settings.externalMcpAllowedTransportsFullLocalAdm: HTTP and stdio external MCP transports can become active in this profile.
settings.externalMcpEffectiveSummary: Effective state: {active} active, {blocked} blocked by policy, {disabled} disabled.
settings.externalMcpLiveHint: Changes are pushed upstream with update_tools while managed MCP WebSocket mode is running.
settings.externalMcpInactiveHint: Changes are saved now and will publish the next time managed MCP WebSocket mode connects.
settings.externalMcpPublishLiveHint: Publish sends the current configuration upstream with update_tools while managed MCP WebSocket mode is running.
settings.externalMcpPublishInactiveHint: Publish saves the current configuration now. update_tools will be sent the next time managed MCP WebSocket mode connects.
settings.externalMcpPublishApplied: Current configuration saved and update_tools published with {toolCount} tool(s).
settings.externalMcpPublishBridgeNotReady: Current configuration saved, but publish is waiting for the managed MCP bridge to become ready. {toolCount} tool(s) are staged.
settings.externalMcpPublishInactive: Current configuration saved, but managed MCP WebSocket mode is not active, so update_tools was not sent.
settings.externalMcpEmpty: No external MCP servers configured yet.
settings.externalMcpRequiredPermissionProfileHint: This server requires at least {required}. The current node profile is {current}.
settings.externalMcpToolsHint: Remote publication requires an explicit allowlist. Test the server first, then select discovered tools below.
settings.externalMcpToolsValidationHint: This list is required and cannot contain '*'. Add explicit tool names only.
settings.externalMcpDiscoverEmpty: Tool discovery finished, but no tools were found for the current configuration.
settings.externalMcpDiscoverBlocked: Tool discovery skipped. {reason} Required profile: {required}. Current profile: {current}.
settings.externalMcpNoDiscoveredTools: Connection succeeded, but no tools were discovered for the current configuration.
settings.externalMcpDisabledWarningTitle: This MCP server is currently disabled
settings.externalMcpDisabledWarningBody: Disabled servers are not loaded locally and their tools are not published or tested until you enable them.
settings.externalMcpRemotePublicationBlockedBody: {reason} This server may still run locally, but its tools will not be published to the remote managed-client session until the configuration is fixed.
settings.externalMcpRemotePublicationDisabledBody: Remote publication is disabled for this server. Current trust level: {trustLevel}. Levels eligible for remote publication: {eligibleLevels}.
settings.externalMcpRemotePublicationTrustLevelTi: Current trust level blocks remote publication
settings.externalMcpRemotePublicationTrustLevelEx: Current trust level: {trustLevel}. This level is intended for local experimentation and is not eligible for remote publication. Levels eligible for remote publication: {eligibleLevels}.
settings.externalMcpRemotePublicationTrustLevelBl: Current trust level: {trustLevel}. This level must not publish tools remotely. Levels eligible for remote publication: {eligibleLevels}.
settings.externalMcpRemotePublicationToolListBody: Current trust level: {trustLevel}. Add an explicit allowed-tools list before publishing this server remotely.
settings.externalMcpRemotePublicationWildcardTitl: Wildcard tools cannot be published remotely
settings.externalMcpRemotePublicationWildcardBody: Current trust level: {trustLevel}. Replace '*' with explicit tool names before publishing this server remotely.
settings.externalMcpTestUnavailableTitle: Testing unavailable for the current node profile
settings.externalMcpTestBlocked: Test skipped. {reason} Required profile: {required}. Current profile: {current}.
settings.externalMcpStatusBlockedTrustLevel: Blocked: trust level is not eligible for remote publication
settings.externalMcpStatusBlockedToolListRequired: Blocked: remote publication requires an explicit tool allowlist
settings.externalMcpStatusBlockedWildcard: Blocked: wildcard tool publication is not allowed remotely
settings.externalMcpDiscoveredToolsHint: Select which of the {count} discovered tool(s) may be published remotely.
settings.externalMcpSaveApplied: Configuration saved and update_tools published with {toolCount} tool(s).
settings.externalMcpSaveItemApplied: Server config saved and hot-reloaded with {toolCount} tool(s).
settings.externalMcpSaveItemBridgeNotReady: Server config saved, but hot reload is waiting for the managed MCP bridge to become ready. {toolCount} tool(s) are staged.
settings.externalMcpSaveItemInactive: Server config saved, but managed MCP WebSocket mode is not active, so hot reload was not applied.
settings.externalMcpSaveOnly: Configuration saved. Tools will publish when managed MCP WebSocket mode is active.
settings.externalMcpRefreshSkipped: Managed MCP WebSocket mode is not active, so update_tools was not sent.
settings.securityNotice: Security guardrails will be available in a future release. For now, the server accepts all connections and commands without restriction. Use with caution.
settings.aboutDescription: CLI Server provides a local WebSocket + HTTP gateway for web-based AI agents to execute CLI commands on your machine. It runs a persistent background service on the configured port and logs all command executions for audit purposes.
settings.aboutDescriptionMcpWs: LandGod Worker is a managed desktop MCP client that connects to a remote MCP Hub via WebSocket. It exposes local tools — shell execution, file access, and external MCP servers — to the remote side under configurable permission profiles, approval gates, and defense layers. All tool calls are audited locally.
settings.languageDescription: Interface language (requires restart for tray menu)
settings.toolCallApprovalDescription: Control whether remote tool calls require manual approval before execution.
settings.toolCallApprovalManualDescription: Each tool call must be manually approved before execution. You can approve once or approve all for the current session.
settings.toolCallApprovalAutoDescription: Tool calls execute automatically without manual confirmation.
settings.toolCallApprovalAutoWarning: Warning: All remote tool calls will execute immediately without your confirmation. Use with caution.
